skill.name: calendly-automation
skill.description: Automate Calendly scheduling, event lookup, invitee review, availability checks, scheduling links, cancellations, and organization administration through Rube MCP.
skill.summary: Guide AI agents through Calendly automation workflows using Rube MCP and current tool schemas.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command execution, weak crypto, and reconnaissance detections are false positives from Markdown inline code, examples, and parameter names in SKILL.md. The real risk is operational: the skill connects to Rube MCP and can read or modify Calendly data, including cancellations and organization membership changes, so publication should include a clear permission warning.

medium_findings.title: External MCP and Calendly Account Access
medium_findings.description: The skill requires a Rube MCP server and an active Calendly OAuth connection. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it exposes calendar and invitee data to external service tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prerequisite and setup instructions explicitly require Rube MCP and Calendly OAuth. The behavior matches the skill purpose, but it creates a real external account access risk.

medium_findings.title: Calendly Destructive Operations Available
medium_findings.description: The skill documents event cancellation, organization invitations, invitation revocation, and member removal. It tells the agent to confirm cancellation, but these operations can still affect users and organizations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed tool sequences include explicit cancellation and organization administration actions. The file also documents safeguards, so this is a controlled but meaningful operational risk.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick detections are Markdown inline code spans for MCP tool names, parameters, and examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command construction was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a Markdown instruction file and the flagged text is enclosed as documentation code spans. No evidence found of shell execution semantics.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts correspond to ordinary documentation text such as sort directions, status examples, API errors, scopes, and rate limits. No cryptographic operation or reconnaissance command was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain scheduling documentation and Calendly API guidance, not code. No evidence found of crypto use, host inspection, or network scanning intent.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Legitimate Service References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs identify the Rube MCP endpoint and Calendly API URI formats needed by the skill. They are not suspicious destinations or hidden exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation for required Calendly and Rube integrations. They align with the skill description and are not used in executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Endpoint Requirement
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to add an external MCP endpoint for Rube and authenticate Calendly through that toolchain. Users should understand what account permissions are granted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and OAuth setup steps are explicit. This is expected functionality, but it is the main trust boundary for the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Calendar and Organization Mutation Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflows can cancel meetings, send organization invitations, revoke invitations, and remove organization members when the connected account has permission.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool names and workflow descriptions directly describe account-modifying actions. The skill includes confirmation guidance for cancellation, reducing but not removing risk.

content.value_statement: Calendly operations often require precise IDs, scopes, pagination, and UTC time handling. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through reliable Rube MCP workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists scheduled Calendly events by user, organization, group, status, date range, and invitee email.
Retrieves event and invitee details using the correct Calendly UUID and URI formats.
Creates single-use scheduling links after resolving the correct event type URI.
Checks event type availability with UTC timestamps and seven-day query windows.
Guides cancellation workflows with event lookup, detail confirmation, and invitee review.
Supports organization invitation, revocation, and member removal workflows for authorized admins.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Calendly OAuth connection before any workflow can run.
Depends on current Rube tool schemas, so agents must search tools before execution.
Organization and group operations require suitable Calendly admin permissions.
Availability checks must use UTC timestamps and may need date range splitting.

use_cases.title: Review Upcoming Meetings
use_cases.description: List scheduled events, inspect details, and check invitees without manually navigating Calendly pages.

use_cases.title: Generate Booking Links
use_cases.description: Find the correct event type, check availability, and create a single-use scheduling link for a client.

use_cases.title: Manage Organization Access
use_cases.description: Review invitations, invite new members, revoke pending invitations, and remove members when authorized.

prompt_templates.title: List My Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Calendly automation skill to list my active events for the next seven days. Show start time, invitee count, and event status.

prompt_templates.title: Find Invitee Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Calendly events for this invitee email, then show the matching event details and invitee status. Use the required user or organization scope.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Scheduling Link
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my active Calendly event types, check available times for the requested week, and create one single-use scheduling link for the selected type.

prompt_templates.title: Administer Organization Invitations
prompt_templates.prompt: Review pending Calendly organization invitations, identify duplicates, and prepare the correct invitation or revocation action for my confirmation.

output_examples.input: Show my active Calendly events for tomorrow.
output_examples.output: A concise event list with meeting names, start times, invitee summaries, status, and pagination notes when more results exist.

output_examples.input: Create a one-time link for my 30 minute discovery call.
output_examples.output: A selected event type, checked availability window, and a single-use scheduling link ready to send.

output_examples.input: Cancel my 2 PM meeting after I confirm.
output_examples.output: The event details, affected invitees, requested cancellation reason, and a final confirmation step before cancellation.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so parameter names and schemas match the current Calendly connector.
Resolve full Calendly user, organization, and event type URIs before list or scheduling workflows.
Ask for explicit user confirmation before cancellations, invitation revocations, or member removals.
anti_patterns: Do not pass me or a bare UUID when a full Calendly API URI is required.
Do not combine user, organization, and group scopes in the same event list request.
Do not run destructive Calendly actions without showing the target record and asking for confirmation.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Calendly connection?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires Rube MCP and an active Calendly OAuth connection before workflows can access account data.

faq.question: Can it create booking links?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the agent to find an event type and create a single-use scheduling link.

faq.question: Can it cancel meetings?
faq.answer: Yes, when the connected account has permission. The skill instructs the agent to confirm details before cancellation.

faq.question: Does it support organization administration?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers organization invitations, revocations, and member removal for authorized Calendly admins.

faq.question: Why must the agent search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the agent use the current parameter names and formats.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The skill uses an external MCP connection with Calendly account permissions, so users should review requested access carefully.
